findings: conventions.md read: ADR-001 (lock-free read-only sweeps) does not constrain describe — it is a read-only command and must be lock-free by the same reasoning.

keel-state.cjs engine read at scripts/keel-state.cjs. Current commands: init, validate, gate, audit, status, snapshot, restore, verify, resume, revert-check, prescan, memory-check. describe is absent.
AGENTS array (line 35-39) maps phase index 0..10 to agent name strings — describe can derive phase names by AGENTS[phase-1] without any new data structure.
cmdStatus (line 446-468) emits raw JSON to stdout and exits 0 or 1 based on sequencing gaps. It must remain byte-for-byte identical — AC-5 regression guard.

readManifest (line 64-68) already emits 'FAIL: no manifest for story <id>' to stderr and exits 1 via die(1,...). describe should delegate to readManifest for the missing-story path, satisfying AC-2 with the existing message.
manifest.updated_at is written on every writeManifest call (line 74) and is the correct basis for idle time per AC-3.
manifest.started_at is set at init time (line 232) — shown as 'Started' in the describe output block.
manifest.halted is a boolean field set by haltPipeline (line 318). Absence means not halted — must treat undefined as false.
manifest.gate_events and manifest.max_gates are tracked (lines 228-229, 347) — 'Gate events used: N / M' is directly available.
Phase file naming convention: NN-<agent>.json where NN is zero-padded phase number (line 273). describe can list completed phases by scanning stateDir for this pattern.
Idle-time formatting: sub-60-minute durations as 'Xm Ys', 60-minute-or-more durations as 'Xh Ym' per AC-3. Both branches must round down (floor) to avoid showing negative seconds.
Zero new npm dependencies confirmed: fs, path already required. No additional require() calls needed beyond what is already in the file.
The describe command must write to stdout (not stderr) on success and exit 0 per AC-1. The separator line and field alignment must be stable for human readability but need not be machine-parseable.
AC-5 scope: 'status --all' output must be unchanged. The cmdStatusAll function (line 475-502) is untouched — no change to JSON shape, sort order, or exit codes.
No CJIS data present in story brief or acceptance criteria. Scope is entirely internal CLI tooling.
decisions: AC-1 confirmed as-is: command prints summary block to stdout, exits 0 when story exists. No human present in session; recorded here per pipeline convention.

AC-2 confirmed as-is: missing story prints 'FAIL: no manifest for story <id>' to stderr, exits 1. The existing readManifest path already satisfies the exact message — describe should reuse it rather than duplicating the error string.
AC-3 confirmed as-is: idle time computed from manifest.updated_at to Date.now(). Format: 'Xh Ym' for durations >= 60 minutes, 'Xm Ys' for durations < 60 minutes. Both values floor-rounded.
AC-4 confirmed as-is: phase names shown using AGENTS[phase-1] lookup — no new data structure needed, AGENTS array already covers all 11 phases.

AC-5 confirmed as-is: cmdStatus and cmdStatusAll are not modified. The regression guard is that the JSON output of both must remain identical — covered by the existing e2e test suite for status --all (KEEL-101/KEEL-102 established baseline).
Decided: describe is lock-free (read-only) consistent with ADR-001. It reads manifest.json and lists phase files — no write path, no lock needed.
Decided: describe output goes to stdout via console.log lines, not a single JSON.stringify — this matches the human-readable intent and diverges from cmdStatus deliberately.

Decided: 'Attempts (this story)' line counts total non-zero entries in manifest.attempts (failures in progress) plus any completed-attempt history visible in the audit log. For the MVP the simpler read from manifest.attempts is sufficient — downstream phases may refine.
Decided: XL split not required — the feature is a single function addition to one file with no schema changes and no new dependencies. Effort estimate is S.
